titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Torch Honor Society, Yale University, Records
Torch Honor Society, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday November 7, 2020 at 09:27

did.unittitle: Torch Honor Society, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of the constitution, member lists, correspondence, minutes,and printed materials documenting initiations, office plans, and general activities of the Torch Honor Society at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 40 (YRG 40), Records of Yale clubs, societies, and organizations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Accessions 19ND-A-484 and 1991-A-089 are open for research. Use of all other accessions requires written permission from the president of the Fax Facis Foundation. Contact a Reference Archivist for more information.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Torch Honor Society, Yale University, Records (RU 72). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for accessions 19ND-A-484 and 1991-A-089 has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

Copyright for all other accessions is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for unpublished works they have authored or otherwise produced. After 120 years from the date of creation or the dissolution of the organization, whichever comes first, copyright passes to Yale University whether such works are at Yale or physically located elsewhere in the world. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Torch Honor Society was founded on March 8, 1916 for the purpose of bestowing recognition of merit and achievement upon deserving students. Each year the society elected ten juniors who were outstanding in various University activities irrespective of society or fraternity affiliation. Their symbol, the uplifted torch, was chosen to signify their allegiance to the Yale creed "Lux et Veritas" (Light and Truth) as well as devotion to their own ideals - enlightening leadership and beneficent service. A broad circle supporting the torch symbolizes equality and comradeship in mutual endeavors, while a third symbol, the Roman numeral X, marks the initial class unit of ten adopted by the founders, and is set across and within the broad circle encompassing the central design. The society selected two professors as charter members, and has conferred honorary titles upon Yale graduates and faculty. The society has re-formed as an organizaton beginning in 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of the constitution, member lists, correspondence, minutes, legal documents, and printed materials documenting initiations, office plans, and general activities of the Torch Honor Society at Yale.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by accession.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Accessions 2016-A-0027, 2017-A-0062, and 2019-A-0026 were gifts of Fax Facis Foundation, 2015, 2017, and 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Non-permanent financial records, including receipts, bank statements, and checkbooks were destroyed in 2008.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: History of the founding of the society
Alumni elections, initiation banquets, and miscellaneous correspondence

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of this material requires written permission from the president of the Fax Facis Foundation. Contact a Reference Archivist for more information.

did.unittitle: Administrative procedures in government agencies documents
Biographical booklets for the delegations of the classes of 2007-2015

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of this material requires written permission from the president of the Fax Facis Foundation. Contact a Reference Archivist for more information.

did.unittitle: Biographical booklets for the delegations of the classes of 2007-2015
Biographical booklets for the delegations of the classes of 2016-2017

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of this material requires written permission from the president of the Fax Facis Foundation. Contact a Reference Archivist for more information.

did.unittitle: Biographical booklets for the delegations of the classes of 2016-2017

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There are two copies of the booklet for each year, one with color images and one in black and white.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series comprises membership and legal documents from to the Torch Honor Society at Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of this material requires the written permission from the president of the Fax Facis Foundation. Contact a Reference Archivist for more information.

did.unittitle: Biographical booklets for the delegation of the Class of 2018

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file includes Bylaws and Articles of Incorporation of Fax Facis Foundation, Inc.
